topic: A musician hears their song being performed by someone else
raw_output:
[story 1]

The cheap headphones felt like a physical weight pressing against my temples. I was nursing a lukewarm coffee, the silence of my cramped apartment amplifying the irritating echo in my head. Then, it started—a melody. It was *my* melody, the one I’d poured three years of sleepless nights into, suddenly being wrenched from my control and spun by someone else’s fingers. My jaw tightened. I watched the grainy livestream feed, my knuckles white where I gripped the mug. The voice that carried the tune was smooth, polished, utterly devoid of the raw, desperate energy I’d infused into every chord. A tight, unpleasant knot formed in my stomach. I leaned back against the peeling wallpaper, letting out a slow, deliberate breath that tasted like ash. *Amateur,* I thought, the word a bitter taste on my tongue. They were taking something precious and making it sound… hollow.

[story 2]

I hadn't expected it. I was scrolling through fan comments, feeling that familiar, dull ache of creative stagnation, when a notification popped up—a live performance from some up-and-coming artist. The moment the opening notes hit, a cold wave washed over me. It was my track. But the interpretation was wrong. They played it with such casual ease, like it was some background noise rather than the emotional core I’d labored over. I shifted uncomfortably on the worn sofa, my gaze fixed on the screen, a faint tremor running through my hands. I took a sharp sip of water, the coolness doing nothing to quell the rising irritation. I wanted to slam my laptop shut, to erase the sound, to deny that this competent stranger had managed to mimic my vulnerability so effortlessly. A low, almost inaudible hum vibrated in my chest—a pure reaction of being thoroughly dismissed.

[story 3]

The studio air was thick with the smell of stale beer and ambition. I was supposed to be reviewing demos, but my attention kept snagging on the monitor. A familiar progression, my signature riff, was being executed live by a band I’d never heard of. It was technically proficient, undeniably catchy, but it lacked the ragged edge, the sheer, painful honesty I’d woven into the arrangement. I stopped pacing. My posture stiffened, every muscle coiled tight. I watched the performer smile, a practiced, easy expression that felt like a deliberate mockery of my own tortured sincerity. I crossed my arms over my chest, a defensive barrier rising. *They don't get it,* I thought, the feeling of being utterly overlooked settling heavy in my chest. I started tapping my foot rapidly against the floor, an impatient, restless rhythm mirroring the frustration bubbling just beneath my skin.
